governing_requirement: Authenticate and replay-protect Main-authored consumer requirements at producer use time, resolve exact trust/schema/authority identities, normalize required versus optional capability envelopes and fail closed on wrong role, revocation, replay, ambiguity or unknown values.

dependency_state.disposition: MF-P6-10.03 remains dependency and implementation blocked. Frozen signed-document and structural-validation foundations receive partial credit; producer admission authority is absent.

prior_evidence.disposition: Accepted for requirement mapping and additive implementation packet. Fresh trusted re-sign probes reproduced its gaps and all governing hashes remain exact.

verified_foundations: The frozen requirements document is strict, canonically hashed and Ed25519-signed by the consumer_requirements role with nonce plus bounded issued/expires timestamps.

It requires the exact twelve schema names, both trust authorities, unique capability IDs, separate required and optional arrays, labels/media/person/transform/authority/resource envelopes and fail-closed runtime flags.
Validation rejects duplicate trust authorities/key IDs, invalid key time ordering, non-exact operational certificate kinds, p50 greater than p95 and request budgets shorter than queue plus p95.
Downstream adoption validation compares the declaration to supplied release and capability snapshots without allowing MaskFactory to author Main intent.
Three focused positive/schema-registry/adoption tests pass and Ruff reports no issues.

direct_probes.gap: No producer use-time, durable nonce/body ledger, current revocation head or prior-decision input can be supplied.

direct_probes.mutation: Change the embedded MaskFactory key_set_sha256 and trusted re-sign.
Replace accepted_mode_a_truth_tiers with invented_super_gold and trusted re-sign.
Give a required capability label unlisted_label while it is absent from required_labels, then trusted re-sign.
Retain a required schema name but replace its schema ID, valid-pattern relative path and SHA-256, then trusted re-sign.

confirmed_gaps: Authentication is evaluated at created_at rather than producer use time, and no durable replay/collision/idempotent-prior-decision ledger is consumed.
Embedded key-set, rotation and revocation identities are not resolved to producer-adopted exact bytes or current role/revocation state.
Authority and truth-tier strings are not normalized through the closed producer authority lattice, so a consumer can name a stronger-sounding unknown tier.
Per-capability modes, labels, artifacts and authority floors are not proven consistent with top-level required/optional envelopes.
Accepted schema name is closed, but ID/path/version/hash are not resolved through an adopted crosswalk and exact producer release catalog.
No canonical admission decision returns deterministic required_met, required_unmet, optional_met and optional_unmet sets with closed reasons.

implementation_packet.path_state: All five additive paths remain absent and clean. Frozen v1 and candidate files are read-only; validation.py remains in the preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty scope.
implementation_packet.authority_boundary: Main authors and signs consumer intent. MaskFactory may validate, admit or reject it but must never fabricate or silently rewrite Main requirements.
implementation_packet.next_action: Hold until MF-P6-09.07 is implemented/adopted and exact-project worker routing is qualified; do not dispatch or duplicate while worker lanes remain disabled.

claim_boundary: This audit credits authenticated Main intent and strict declaration structure only. It does not implement producer use-time/replay admission, resolve trust/schema/authority identities, publish or adopt Main requirements, complete MF-P6-09.07, or complete MF-P6-10.03.
